regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-07-03] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 2.1% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.67% YoY | core CPI 2.26% | core PCE 2.89% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.2%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.48% | 10Y-2Y 0.35% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 44.8 | VIX 16.59

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ (as of 2026-07-03 ~17:24 UTC). NOTE UP FRONT: This is intelligence, not a validated edge. My own scorecard is n=11 (hit 0.818, regime acc 0.273) and explicitly CALIBRATING — treat the direction-call as informed opinion, not proven signal. Also note today is an NFP day (8:30 ET, high-impact) — that alone caps confidence.

1) RECENT MOVE-BY-MOTE READ. Both indices are constructively higher: ES +0.38% on the day / +2.1% on the week; NQ +1.17% on the day / +1.82% on the week. Both sit comfortably above their 200-day SMAs (ES +8.3%, NQ +14.0%), so the primary trend is up. Interestingly NQ led on the day (+1.17% vs ES +0.38%) but ES led on the week — a mild tech re-acceleration into the print without ES lagging badly. RSI is telling: ES 58.5 (firm, momentum intact, not overbought) while NQ is only 51.7 (neutral) despite the bigger daily pop — meaning NQ bounced off a softer base and has more room before stretched. Likely catalysts: the dominant news thread is FED_RATES ("US jobs data changes Warsh's game") tying today's action to NFP-driven rate-path repositioning; geopolitics/energy headlines (Hormuz oil flows, Iran attack chatter) are ambient risk but not driving a clean directional impulse. Most tagged headlines are low-signal noise (cotton, local India stories) — do not weight them.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ. ES: POC 7553.04 sits right below spot (7557) with a tight cluster of high-volume nodes (7553, 7548, 7557, 7527, 7498) — price is trading AT the fair-value POC, and value-area high is 7565.61. So ES is pinned at the top of its 5-day value area — acceptance above 7565.61 opens continuation; rejection there sends it back toward VAL 7460.82. NQ: POC 30167.7 sits ABOVE spot (29901.75), and value-area runs 29394–30539. NQ is trading in the lower-middle of its value area, below its POC — meaning NQ has an unfilled magnet overhead near 30,168 but is not yet in acceptance up there. Line in the sand (NQ): session VWAP 29773 and the shelf near VAL 29394 below; overhead POC 30168 and prior-day high 30320 above. Reminder: this is volume-AT-price (where trade happened), NOT order-flow delta — I can't confirm buy/sell aggression, so treat POC as a magnet, not proof of accumulation.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME. Clean BREADTH read. Contemporaneous correlation 0.936, best cross-lag correlation only 0.043 (leader = NONE), divergence = FALSE. ES and NQ are moving together, no meaningful lead-lag edge, and the return spread is essentially flat (20d avg -0.0005). Implication: this is the MORE durable, more trustworthy regime — when they agree, direction is more reliable, so you can trust the trend direction slightly more and don't need to fear a hidden rotation. Sizing implication favors normal-to-modest confidence in the up-trend continuation — BUT regime is an INPUT, not an edge, and my regime-accuracy track record (0.273) argues for humility on this classification specifically.

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS. NONFARM PAYROLLS TODAY, 8:30 ET, high-impact. This is the single most important line in this briefing: widen stops, size DOWN into the print, and do not chase the first spike — NFP first-moves routinely reverse. CPI is ~9 days out (07-12) — not today's problem but reduce size into that window later. Given NFP is live and ATRs are wide (ES ATR14 ~99pts, NQ ATR14 ~769pts), expect large whippy ranges; position for post-print acceptance rather than pre-print positioning.

5) SYNTHESIS. Meta-regime: durable BREADTH uptrend, both indices above 200SMA, ES pinned at POC/value-area high, NQ with an unfilled magnet overhead — constructive but event-gated. Narrowed bias: LONG / long-the-dip, ~58% confidence, favoring the trend and the overhead NQ POC magnet (30,168) as the upside target. INVALIDATION: below 29,773 (NQ session VWAP) on acceptance turns this neutral-to-cautious; a decisive break below 29,394 (NQ VAL) flips the bias short and invalidates the long entirely. Confidence is deliberately capped at 58 — not higher — because although lead-lag is clean (BREADTH), the news is noisy/mixed and NFP is TODAY, which is precisely the condition where I should NOT manufacture certainty. Trade small into 8:30, let the print define acceptance, then align with the breadth trend.
